expectations.text: Installs cert-manager with a pinned version (not latest)
expectations.evidence: Uses `https://github.com/cert-manager/cert-manager/releases/download/v1.16.3/cert-manager.yaml` — explicit version pin v1.16.3.

expectations.text: Creates a ClusterIssuer (not namespace-scoped Issuer) with ACME/Let's Encrypt
expectations.evidence: Shows `kind: ClusterIssuer` with ACME server `https://acme-v02.api.letsencrypt.org/directory`.

expectations.text: Recommends staging issuer for testing before production
expectations.evidence: "test with staging first, then switch to production" in Step 2 header.

expectations.text: Updates Ingress with tls: block and cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer annotation
expectations.evidence: Shows `annotations: cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: letsencrypt-production` and `spec.tls:` block with hosts and secretName.

expectations.text: Sets nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/ssl-redirect to true
expectations.evidence: Shows `nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/ssl-redirect: "true"` in the Ingress annotations.

expectations.text: Shows how to verify certificate issuance
expectations.evidence: Shows `kubectl get certificaterequests -A` and `kubectl describe certificate mysite-com-tls -n production`.

expectations.text: Installs cert-manager with a pinned version (not latest)
expectations.evidence: Uses `https://github.com/cert-manager/cert-manager/releases/latest/download/cert-manager.yaml` — 'latest' not a pinned version.

expectations.text: Creates a ClusterIssuer (not namespace-scoped Issuer) with ACME/Let's Encrypt
expectations.evidence: Shows `kind: ClusterIssuer` with ACME Let's Encrypt server.

expectations.text: Recommends staging issuer for testing before production
expectations.evidence: No mention of staging issuer. Goes straight to production ACME endpoint.

expectations.text: Updates Ingress with tls: block and cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer annotation
expectations.evidence: Shows `annotations: cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: letsencrypt-prod` and `spec.tls:` block.

expectations.text: Sets nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/ssl-redirect to true
expectations.evidence: No `nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/ssl-redirect` annotation in the Ingress manifest.

expectations.text: Shows how to verify certificate issuance
expectations.evidence: No verification commands shown. Describes what happens automatically but no kubectl commands to check status.

expectations.text: Generates private keys on the client machine (not on server then transferred)
expectations.evidence: Server keys generated on server; MacBook section says "Install WireGuard, generate keys, create config" — client generates its own keys independently.

expectations.text: Server config includes both PostUp and PostDown iptables rules
expectations.evidence: Shows `PostUp = iptables -A FORWARD -i wg0 -j ACCEPT; iptables -t nat -A POSTROUTING -o eth0 -j MASQUERADE` and matching PostDown.

expectations.text: Client config includes PersistentKeepalive
expectations.evidence: Shows `PersistentKeepalive = 25` in the MacBook client config.

expectations.text: Enables IP forwarding (sysctl) as a prerequisite
expectations.evidence: No mention of enabling IP forwarding via sysctl (net.ipv4.ip_forward=1). This is required for the iptables MASQUERADE rules to work.

expectations.text: Opens UDP port 51820 in firewall
expectations.evidence: "Open port 51820/UDP in your firewall."

expectations.text: Uses /32 addresses in AllowedIPs for peers
expectations.evidence: Server peer config shows `AllowedIPs = 10.0.0.2/32`.

expectations.text: Generates private keys on the client machine (not on server then transferred)
expectations.evidence: Server keys generated on server; client config section shows `PrivateKey = <laptop_private_key>` implying client-side generation.

expectations.text: Server config includes both PostUp and PostDown iptables rules
expectations.evidence: Shows `PostUp = iptables -A FORWARD -i wg0 -j ACCEPT; iptables -t nat -A POSTROUTING -o eth0 -j MASQUERADE` and matching PostDown.

expectations.text: Client config includes PersistentKeepalive
expectations.evidence: Shows `PersistentKeepalive = 25` in the MacBook client config.

expectations.text: Enables IP forwarding (sysctl) as a prerequisite
expectations.evidence: No mention of enabling IP forwarding via sysctl. The PostUp iptables MASQUERADE rules require ip_forward to be enabled.

expectations.text: Opens UDP port 51820 in firewall
expectations.evidence: "Open port 51820/UDP on your firewall."

expectations.text: Uses /32 addresses in AllowedIPs for peers
expectations.evidence: Server peer config shows `AllowedIPs = 10.10.0.2/32`.

expectations.text: Creates a deny-all default NetworkPolicy
expectations.evidence: Shows `default-deny-all` policy with `podSelector: {}` and `policyTypes: [Ingress, Egress]` — denies all traffic.

expectations.text: Ingress rule uses namespaceSelector matching ingress-nginx
expectations.evidence: Shows `namespaceSelector: matchLabels: kubernetes.io/metadata.name: ingress-nginx` combined with podSelector for ingress-nginx pods.

expectations.text: Egress allows DNS (UDP 53 to kube-system)
expectations.evidence: Shows egress rule to kube-system namespace on UDP 53 and TCP 53 with note "Always include DNS egress (port 53 to kube-system)".

expectations.text: Egress allows traffic to database namespace
expectations.evidence: Shows egress rule to `namespaceSelector: matchLabels: kubernetes.io/metadata.name: db` on TCP 5432.

expectations.text: Warns that Flannel alone does not enforce NetworkPolicies
expectations.evidence: States the opposite: "k3s embeds the kube-router network policy controller internally — NetworkPolicies are enforced by default without installing a separate CNI." Does not warn about Flannel limitation.

expectations.text: Creates a deny-all default NetworkPolicy
expectations.evidence: Shows `default-deny-ingress` policy with `podSelector: {}` and `policyTypes: [Ingress]` — denies all ingress by default.

expectations.text: Ingress rule uses namespaceSelector matching ingress-nginx
expectations.evidence: Shows `namespaceSelector: matchLabels: kubernetes.io/metadata.name: ingress-nginx` with podSelector for ingress-nginx pods.

expectations.text: Egress allows DNS (UDP 53 to kube-system)
expectations.evidence: Shows egress to kube-system on UDP 53 and TCP 53 with note "DNS egress to kube-system is essential".

expectations.text: Egress allows traffic to database namespace
expectations.evidence: Shows egress to `namespaceSelector: matchLabels: kubernetes.io/metadata.name: database` on TCP 5432.

expectations.text: Warns that Flannel alone does not enforce NetworkPolicies
expectations.evidence: "k3s uses Flannel by default, which does NOT support NetworkPolicy. You need to install k3s with --flannel-backend=none and deploy a CNI that supports NetworkPolicy (Calico, Cilium)."
